note: Framework overlays are heuristic translations for AI and replay analysis. They supplement existing strategy cards and do not replace base doctrine.

overlays.title: NQ Europe Large Offer Breakdown
overlays.price_action: Repeated failure above a ceiling transitions into clean downside expansion away from that ceiling.
overlays.order_flow: Offer absorption and reloads keep capping price until initiative sellers gain real downside travel.
overlays.ict_smc: Buy-side liquidity near the highs is raided and bearish displacement leaves the local supply area in control.
overlays.chanlun: A failed upward departure from a local center flips into a continuation sell segment.
overlays.main_trap: Shorting too early while the market is still rotating under the cap without accepted downside travel.
overlays.ai_focus: Did the cap actually produce lower acceptance, or only visual heaviness without real release?

overlays.title: NQ Europe Offer Reversal Into Upper Liquidity
overlays.price_action: A failed breakdown converts into reversal structure toward upper liquidity.
overlays.order_flow: Sellers fail to gain lower acceptance and responsive buyers reclaim the path higher.
overlays.ict_smc: Sell-side liquidity is swept first, then bullish shift and displacement target buy-side liquidity above.
overlays.chanlun: The lower test behaves like a failed sell departure and a second-buy style recovery through the center.
overlays.main_trap: Buying before the reclaim is visible or buying directly into an intact upper suppressor.
overlays.ai_focus: Was the lower sweep rejected strongly enough to justify a reversal script toward upper liquidity?

overlays.title: NQ Failed Overhead Capping In Ascent
overlays.price_action: Overhead supply creates only a pause before trend continuation.
overlays.order_flow: The cap absorbs briefly, yet buyers retain initiative and the counter-response never deepens enough to reverse control.
overlays.ict_smc: A bearish order-block style cap fails to deliver reversal and becomes a continuation checkpoint.
overlays.chanlun: The rising segment has not shown real bearish divergence, so the pause stays corrective rather than terminal.
overlays.main_trap: Fading the first visible cap before deeper acceptance lower is proven.
overlays.ai_focus: Did the cap force deeper acceptance lower, or did buyers quickly re-engage and preserve continuation?

overlays.title: NQ Probe Reversal Toward Upper Liquidity
overlays.price_action: A failed downside probe transitions into upward reclaim and rotation.
overlays.order_flow: Aggressive selling cannot extend and responsive buyers reclaim the path higher after the probe.
overlays.ict_smc: A sell-side sweep turns into bullish shift and displacement toward upper draw-on-liquidity.
overlays.chanlun: The failed probe behaves like a weakening down segment with second-buy style recovery after structure is reclaimed.
overlays.main_trap: Treating the first probe as a full reversal before reclaim evidence exists.
overlays.ai_focus: Did the probe actually fail with reclaim evidence, or was it only a brief bounce inside ongoing downside acceptance?

overlays.title: NQ Replenished Bid Launchpad Into Upper Liquidity
overlays.price_action: A defended low forms the base for continuation once higher lows begin to print.
overlays.order_flow: Same-price bid replenishment absorbs sell pressure until buyers can lift away from the defended zone.
overlays.ict_smc: A discount-area defense or bullish order block holds while sell-side liquidity fails to break it.
overlays.chanlun: A lower center edge holds and can produce second-buy or third-buy style continuation.
overlays.main_trap: Buying from one visible bid or buying in auction middle without edge location.
overlays.ai_focus: Was there true same-price defense with structure reclaim, or only a large bid without reliable hold quality?

overlays.title: NQ Upper Liquidity Harvest Then Lower Relocation
overlays.price_action: After target completion, the market balances, pulls back, or relocates lower.
overlays.order_flow: The buy drive exhausts at target and post-harvest delta quality weakens.
overlays.ict_smc: Buy-side liquidity is taken and attention shifts to premium exhaustion, distribution, or reversal watch.
overlays.chanlun: An upward segment reaches objective and then risks divergence and return toward the center.
overlays.main_trap: Continuing to chase longs after the primary objective has already been harvested.
overlays.ai_focus: After harvest, did the market only pause, or did it begin a genuine lower relocation process?

overlays.title: NQ Gap Fill Balance Reclaim
overlays.price_action: Gap repair matters only if price can also re-enter and hold prior balance or value.
overlays.order_flow: A fill is just the path; the real information is whether post-fill trade is accepted or rejected.
overlays.ict_smc: The gap behaves like imbalance repair, but reclaim of the old dealing range is the real signal.
overlays.chanlun: Price first leaves the prior center, then tests whether it can return and stabilize back inside it.
overlays.main_trap: Trading the gap fill itself rather than the hold quality after the fill.
overlays.ai_focus: Did the fill become true balance reclaim, or did price only touch the gap objective and reject?

overlays.title: NQ Manipulation Leg Context
overlays.price_action: A true manipulation leg is a forcing move that changes later structure, not just a random fast bar sequence.
overlays.order_flow: The move must show genuine displacement from origin and matter for later continuation, trap, or distribution logic.
overlays.ict_smc: This is the displacement leg that sweeps liquidity and defines the next draw-on-liquidity narrative.
overlays.chanlun: It resembles a dominant segment that resets structural expectation for the next move.
overlays.main_trap: Labeling every sharp impulse as a manipulation leg when it started in the middle of balance.
overlays.ai_focus: Did this leg truly reset the script, or was it just a fast bar sequence inside noise?

overlays.title: NQ Measured Move Ladder Context
overlays.price_action: Extension should be measured against a meaningful prior unit and not forced inside random chop.
overlays.order_flow: The move deserves ladder logic only when initiative and body confirmation keep validating the extension.
overlays.ict_smc: This is distance-to-liquidity tracking after displacement, not a guarantee that every multiple must be hit.
overlays.chanlun: It is a segment-length comparison tool for judging continuation quality versus exhaustion.
overlays.main_trap: Applying measured-move logic after the market has already shifted back into balance.
overlays.ai_focus: Is the measured ladder still alive in trend context, or is the market already transitioning into exhaustion or balance?

overlays.title: Large Order Lifecycle Diagnosis
overlays.price_action: A level is only meaningful if revisits still produce usable reaction quality.
overlays.order_flow: Separate defended size, trapped size, unwinding size, continuing size, and spoof-risk behavior.
overlays.ict_smc: An order block is valid only if it still creates displacement or defense; otherwise it can degrade or flip.
overlays.chanlun: A key center edge or segment origin only stays important if revisits still confirm it structurally.
overlays.main_trap: Trusting displayed size without diagnosing its lifecycle branch.
overlays.ai_focus: Is the large order still defending, already trapped, unwinding, or merely visual bait?

overlays.title: Trapped Inventory Release
overlays.price_action: The failed level becomes fuel once the wrong side cannot recover.
overlays.order_flow: Acceptance beyond the failure point plus failed reclaim converts trapped inventory into forced exit energy.
overlays.ict_smc: The original breakout or defense fails, then the broken area behaves like a breaker as trapped holders fuel the opposite move.
overlays.chanlun: A failed departure from the center flips and accelerates into the opposite segment once rescue attempts fail.
overlays.main_trap: Calling inventory trapped before price has truly accepted beyond the failure point.
overlays.ai_focus: Where did the trapped side become objectively wrong, and did failed reclaim confirm the release?

overlays.title: Failed Break Acceptance Re-Entry
overlays.price_action: A breakout becomes a failure only after price returns inside and re-accepts the prior auction.
overlays.order_flow: Outside hold collapses, re-entry is accepted, and rotation back through the old area becomes likely.
overlays.ict_smc: The initial break behaves like a false BOS or sweep, and the real signal is accepted trade back inside range.
overlays.chanlun: The move fails to stay outside the center and returns into the center instead of starting a durable new segment.
overlays.main_trap: Fading the first break before re-entry is accepted.
overlays.ai_focus: Was the re-entry actually accepted back inside the old auction, or only touched briefly?

overlays.title: Control Handoff After Harvest
overlays.price_action: After the target is hit, determine whether the pullback is only a pause or the first real handoff.
overlays.order_flow: The previous winner loses control only if it cannot defend the first meaningful revisit after harvest.
overlays.ict_smc: After external liquidity is taken, watch whether the old side still owns continuation or whether a new side takes control.
overlays.chanlun: An objective-complete segment transitions into the opposite side only if a new segment can actually dominate.
overlays.main_trap: Forcing a major reversal immediately after harvest without proof of handoff.
overlays.ai_focus: Did the old winner fail its first defense after harvest, or is the market only pausing before continuation?

overlays.title: False Liquidity And Pull Behavior
overlays.price_action: Visible walls mean little if they do not survive the approach and create actual reaction.
overlays.order_flow: The key distinction is refill and hold near touch versus flashing and pulling before touch.
overlays.ict_smc: This is inducement and false liquidity presentation, not genuine defense, until the order behaves like a defended level.
overlays.chanlun: Treat it as a false edge rather than a real center boundary until structure confirms it.
overlays.main_trap: Trading displayed size as if it were already bona fide support or resistance.
overlays.ai_focus: Did the displayed size survive near touch and defend, or did it mostly flash and pull?

overlays.title: No-Trade Garbage Time Context
overlays.price_action: Many active candles inside the middle of a wider auction still do not create real edge.
overlays.order_flow: Repeated micro-events without durable acceptance usually mean noise, recycling, and poor asymmetry.
overlays.ict_smc: Internal range inducement without displacement or clear liquidity draw should suppress entries.
overlays.chanlun: This is center-inside oscillation rather than a valid departure or return setup.
overlays.main_trap: Inventing a trade because the market is moving inside non-edge territory.
overlays.ai_focus: Is there a real edge boundary and accepted branch, or only event noise inside a larger auction?

overlays.title: Cross-Session Inventory Transfer
overlays.price_action: A prior-session zone can stay active and govern the next session's reaction or release.
overlays.order_flow: Old inventory can be defended, unwound, inherited, or weaponized by the next session.
overlays.ict_smc: Prior-session liquidity pools and dealing ranges remain active reference points for the next session handoff.
overlays.chanlun: A previous segment center or edge can continue to organize the next session's internal structure.
overlays.main_trap: Treating each session as isolated when inventory is obviously being transferred.
overlays.ai_focus: Is the next session accepting new control, or merely processing inherited inventory from the prior session?

overlays.title: Opening Inventory Correction Vs Continuation
overlays.price_action: The open often first corrects overnight imbalance before the true session direction appears.
overlays.order_flow: The first drive only matters if it can hold acceptance instead of acting as emotional opening flow.
overlays.ict_smc: This is the opening raid versus true session displacement distinction, not a blind reaction to the first burst.
overlays.chanlun: The first opening segment may only be a corrective sub-segment rather than the start of a major directional segment.
overlays.main_trap: Chasing the first opening burst without knowing whether it is correction or continuation.
overlays.ai_focus: Was the opening burst real session control, or only overnight inventory being corrected?

overlays.title: Anchor Market Leadership
overlays.price_action: Trade from the clearer chart when one related market is structurally cleaner than the other.
overlays.order_flow: The better anchor is the market that shows cleaner acceptance or rejection first.
overlays.ict_smc: Correlated-market confirmation is useful only when the anchor market truly leads the narrative.
overlays.chanlun: The cleaner segment structure should guide interpretation while the noisier market remains secondary.
overlays.main_trap: Forcing the noisier or lagging market into leadership just because it is moving faster.
overlays.ai_focus: Which related market is actually clearer right now, and did the lagger genuinely confirm or only follow late?

overlays.title: Leader Failure Divergence Trap
overlays.price_action: Once the prior leader fails, the lagger often becomes a trap for late followers.
overlays.order_flow: Divergence becomes actionable only after the leader clearly loses hold quality.
overlays.ict_smc: The leader's failed break or failed displacement invalidates the lagger's late confirmation story.
overlays.chanlun: The leading segment is already weakening or ending while the lagging market still extends the old story.
overlays.main_trap: Continuing to trust lagger follow-through after the leader has already failed.
overlays.ai_focus: Has the leader already invalidated the move while the lagger still invites late confirmation?

overlays.title: Liquidity Raid Vs Breakout Acceptance
overlays.price_action: The sweep itself is not the trade; the hold quality after the sweep is the trade.
overlays.order_flow: Stop release creates the burst, but continuation or failure is decided by outside hold and the first pullback.
overlays.ict_smc: This is the difference between a liquidity sweep and a true accepted BOS or continuation displacement.
overlays.chanlun: The move only counts as a real departure from the center if it can stand outside; otherwise it returns to the center.
overlays.main_trap: Chasing the first sweep or fading it before outside hold quality is resolved.
overlays.ai_focus: Did the market convert the raid into accepted outside trade, or did it only sweep liquidity and return?

overlays.title: Forced Exit Burst Vs Initiative Continuation
overlays.price_action: A squeeze or flush can be a one-shot release or the beginning of true continuation.
overlays.order_flow: Once trapped exits are mostly done, the new side still needs to defend gained ground and re-accelerate.
overlays.ict_smc: Short covering or long liquidation is not automatically true displacement continuation by fresh initiative.
overlays.chanlun: Forced acceleration can be exhaustion-prone if no new segment continuity appears after the release.
overlays.main_trap: Confusing trapped-side exit fuel with durable initiative takeover.
overlays.ai_focus: After the burst, did the new side defend and re-accelerate, or was the move mostly exit fuel?

overlays.title: Post-Shock Resiliency Check
overlays.price_action: The shock itself is not the edge; the first readable pullback decides rebuild versus repair.
overlays.order_flow: Post-shock hold quality and rebuilt depth matter more than the initiating burst.
overlays.ict_smc: After displacement or liquidity take, the real question is whether the new side can keep the new dealing range.
overlays.chanlun: A burst only matters if a new segment can continue after the first corrective pause.
overlays.main_trap: Chasing the shock instead of reading the branch after it.
overlays.ai_focus: After the event, did the market rebuild under the new side or repair back toward balance?

overlays.title: Fake Depth Signature Context
overlays.price_action: Visible walls matter only if they survive the approach and create actual reaction.
overlays.order_flow: Near-touch cancellation and opposite-side real fills reveal unreliable depth.
overlays.ict_smc: This is inducement by displayed liquidity rather than genuine support or resistance.
overlays.chanlun: Treat it as a false edge until structure proves a real center boundary.
overlays.main_trap: Trusting brightness instead of survival and outcome.
overlays.ai_focus: Did the displayed depth actually survive near touch and defend, or did it disappear when pressure arrived?

overlays.title: Stop-Cluster Cascade Exhaustion
overlays.price_action: A violent stop release is only useful after outside hold quality is known.
overlays.order_flow: Stop fuel can create distance quickly, but continuation needs rebuild after the first retest.
overlays.ict_smc: A liquidity sweep and cascade are not automatically accepted BOS continuation.
overlays.chanlun: Forced acceleration can end as exhaustion if no new segment continuity appears after the sweep.
overlays.main_trap: Assuming violent extension automatically means durable continuation.
overlays.ai_focus: After the cascade, did outside trade hold and rebuild, or did the release exhaust into repair?

overlays.title: Order-Flow Imbalance Without Price Progress
overlays.price_action: Loud tape without travel usually signals absorption, stall, or weakening edge.
overlays.order_flow: Same-side aggression is not enough if price progress per burst keeps deteriorating.
overlays.ict_smc: Displacement is not real if imbalance cannot clear the nearby boundary or keep extending.
overlays.chanlun: The segment loses efficiency when repeated effort no longer creates actual departure.
overlays.main_trap: Trading delta color or imbalance count instead of actual price progress.
overlays.ai_focus: Is the aggression still producing travel, or is the market absorbing it at the same boundary?

overlays.title: Liquidity Provider Retreat Under Toxicity
overlays.price_action: A previously real defended level can lose authority once the defender reprices under pressure.
overlays.order_flow: Passive size may retreat or stand deeper when adverse-selection pressure rises.
overlays.ict_smc: This is not always fake depth; sometimes the old order block or defense price simply loses viability under toxic flow.
overlays.chanlun: The old center edge loses authority once the defending side shifts to a new reference price.
overlays.main_trap: Treating every retreat as spoofing or still trusting the old price after repricing.
overlays.ai_focus: Did the provider actually change state and defend a new price, or is the old price still the real reference?

overlays.title: Initial Balance Extension Vs True Breakout
overlays.price_action: An IB extension is only the first observation; a true breakout requires accepted outside trade after it.
overlays.order_flow: Outside hold quality and the first retest matter more than the first poke beyond IB.
overlays.ict_smc: An opening-range extension is not real breakout acceptance until the market keeps the new dealing range outside IB.
overlays.chanlun: A move beyond the opening center only matters if it can stand outside instead of returning into the old opening structure.
overlays.main_trap: Promoting the first IB extension into a breakout without accepted outside trade.
overlays.ai_focus: Did the market only extend beyond IB, or did it actually accept outside and build there?

overlays.title: Gap Bar Retracement Continuation
overlays.price_action: A clean displacement becomes tradable on the first disciplined pullback, not on emotional chase after the impulse bar.
overlays.order_flow: Initiative wins the first release, then the key read is whether the pullback stays shallow enough to prove continuation demand still owns the lane.
overlays.ict_smc: This behaves like a displacement leg followed by a qualified mitigation into the reaction zone before continuation resumes.
overlays.chanlun: A fresh departure segment stays valid only if the first pullback remains corrective instead of collapsing back into the old center.
overlays.main_trap: Treating late recycled retests or overlapping chop as if they were the first clean retracement after true displacement.
overlays.ai_focus: Was the impulse genuinely clean and is this still the first pullback, or has the move already recycled back toward balance?

overlays.title: Major Trend Reversal Trap Confirmation
overlays.price_action: A reversal becomes real when the prior breakout side is trapped and confirmation closes beyond the level they needed to defend.
overlays.order_flow: The reversal edge is not the signal bar alone but the failed rescue of trapped participants after their breakout loses hold quality.
overlays.ict_smc: This is a false breakout or failed continuation that turns into real market-structure shift only after confirmation through the trapped side's origin zone.
overlays.chanlun: The prior segment fails to extend, then a confirmed opposite segment begins only after the old side cannot recover its critical defense point.
overlays.main_trap: Front-running the reversal before trapped breakout traders are objectively wrong and before confirmation proves the rescue attempt failed.
overlays.ai_focus: Who is trapped, where did they become wrong, and did the confirmation close actually remove their rescue path?

overlays.title: Mother Bar Auction Zones
overlays.price_action: A mother bar is an internal auction map whose edges behave differently in ranges, early trends, and late exhaustion.
overlays.order_flow: The useful information is whether outer mother-bar zones attract rejection, breakout acceptance, or only dead-space rotation through the middle.
overlays.ict_smc: The mother bar acts like a compact dealing range where edge reaction matters more than the visual pattern itself.
overlays.chanlun: This is a local center-within-center structure, and the real edge comes from how price responds at its boundary rather than in its middle.
overlays.main_trap: Opening from the middle of the mother bar or fading an edge after outside trade has already become accepted.
overlays.ai_focus: Is price testing an outer mother-bar boundary with context support, or is it just rotating in the middle without real edge?

overlays.title: Caveman Failed Signal Bar Trigger
overlays.price_action: A strong breakout or continuation bar is immediately reversed by an even stronger opposite trigger bar.
overlays.order_flow: Aggressive participation on the signal bar gets trapped immediately, and the opposite side exploits the failed move before rescue can form.
overlays.ict_smc: This behaves like a false breakout or failed continuation where the decisive clue is immediate displacement back through the signal bar control point.
overlays.chanlun: A fresh attempted departure fails instantly and flips into a fast opposite sub-segment before the old side can stabilize.
overlays.main_trap: Treating any two-bar reversal as Caveman when the signal bar was weak or the trigger did not truly negate it.
overlays.ai_focus: Was there a real committed signal bar that got fully negated, or is this only visual bar drama inside noise?

overlays.title: H2 L2 Pullback Continuation Framework
overlays.price_action: A second-entry pullback is valuable only when the trend is still intact and the pullback remains corrective.
overlays.order_flow: The first countertrend push fails, then the second continuation attempt tests whether the dominant side still owns initiative from a meaningful location.
overlays.ict_smc: This is continuation after mitigation, not blind counting, and the best versions reclaim from EMA, breakout point, or structural support-resistance.
overlays.chanlun: A corrective sub-segment ends and the main segment attempts to resume, but only while the original structural origin still holds.
overlays.main_trap: Counting endlessly inside chop and calling it H2 or L2 after the market has already degraded into range behavior.
overlays.ai_focus: Is this still a valid corrective pullback in trend context, or has the market already become broad, overlapping, and count-invalid?
